title: Corrective fix for B-b0981c8e-test: Test Detect planner-provided shared followups in scheduler
description:
Blocked reason: Existing scheduler validation still fails: `tests.test_orchestrator.DeleteBeadCliTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children` shows legacy followup children are still being created alongside planner-owned shared followups.

Remaining work: Developer follow-up is required in `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` so planner-provided shared followups fully suppress legacy `-test`/`-docs`/`-review` child creation in the existing scheduler flow, then rerun `uv run python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator.DeleteBeadCliTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children -v` and the new regression test.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B-b0981c8e-test.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented the corrective fix in `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` by short-circuiting legacy followup creation when a developer bead is in planner-owned followup mode and at least one shared followup already exists outside the developer bead. Added regression coverage in `tests/test_orchestrator.py` to ensure the scheduler does not backfill legacy children when a planner-owned shared tester bead exists. Verified syntax/import health with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py tests/test_orchestrator.py` and `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Tester agent should rerun `uv run python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator.DeleteBeadCliTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children -v` and the new regression test, then retry the parent bead if those pass.
handoff_summary.risks: No test suite was run in this pass per developer guardrails. The new behavior suppresses all legacy followup backfill once planner-owned shared followups are detected for a developer bead, so tester coverage should confirm that matches the intended planner/scheduler contract across adjacent followup scenarios.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run the targeted scheduler/unit tests for planner-owned shared followups, then retry bead `B-b0981c8e-test` if validation passes.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Scheduler followup creation semantics are now stricter: any detected planner-owned shared followup for a developer bead suppresses legacy child creation for that bead. Downstream agents should validate only the shared-followup path and avoid assuming mixed shared-plus-legacy followups will still be synthesized.

conflict_risks: Scheduler followup creation semantics are now stricter: any detected planner-owned shared followup for a developer bead suppresses legacy child creation for that bead. Downstream agents should validate only the shared-followup path and avoid assuming mixed shared-plus-legacy followups will still be synthesized.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
